Patch Set 3:

You should update http://wiki.lustre.org/Lustre_Coding_Guidelines to match.

This is actually sad, since I've definitely seen bugs in patches where "if (!ptr ... )" or "if (!val ...)" are used in complex expressions.

Tests uploaded by Shadow Autotest2 from shadow-55vm5 for review-zfs-part-2 received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.7/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/80c0373a-d06d-11e5-be99-5254006e85c2). Ran 8 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 14:

(3 comments)

Looking at the upstream kernel, I see over 30k cases of "if (foo == NULL)" or "if (foo != NULL)" and over 58k cases of "if (bar == 0)" or "if (bar != 0)", so I'm less inclined to believe that this is a requirement for moving out of staging.

While I'm ready to accept patches that have already been landed upstream for the sake of code unification, I'd rather avoid wholesale changes like this driven by us that IMHO actually make the code worse.

I'd rather focus on productive changes like removing wrappers that obscure callpaths like TGT_RPC_HANDLER() and OCD_HAS_FLAG() and LDLM_HAVE_MASK() that actually make the code harder to follow.

Not sure what Oleg thinks of this.

Patch Set 14: Code-Review-1

(65 comments)

While I'm definitely not thrilled with this style change, in many of the cases the change is acceptable.  Some of them are actively making the code harder to read and need to be reverted.  In other cases, some code reorg would return the code to a readable state and not have double negatives to try and mentally parse.

parents.subject: LU-6142 lnet: non functionality cleanups for lnet

commit.subject: LU-6142 lnet: handle NULL and zero comparison correctly
commit.message:
LU-6142 lnet: handle NULL and zero comparison correctly

For the linux kernel the test if (blah == NULL) and
if (rc == 0) are considered bad style. This patch
converts it to the if (blah) style that is required.

Change-Id: Ie10f630e136fbc7092c688b09d3eeb381aedd3dd
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
